title: Yellow Gravy (Yogurt-Besan Gravy)
description: Yellow Gravy is a classic Indian curry base that uses yogurt and gram flour (besan) to create a rich, tangy, and creamy gravy. This gravy is often used with vegetables or paneer.

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Red chili powder — ½ tsp (adjust to taste)

instruction.title: Step 1: Prepare the Yogurt-Besan Mixture
steps: In a bowl, whisk the yogurt well until smooth and lump-free.
Add the besan (gram flour) and mix thoroughly to combine.
The mixture should be smooth without any lumps.

instruction.title: Step 2: Cook the Besan
steps: Heat oil or ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add a pinch of asafoetida and the slit green chili. Sauté for a few seconds.
Add ginger paste and sauté until raw smell disappears (about 30 seconds).

instruction.title: Step 3: Add Spices
steps: Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, and coriander powder.
Stir quickly to avoid burning the spices.

instruction.title: Step 4: Cook the Yogurt-Besan Mix
steps: Reduce the flame to low.
Slowly add the yogurt-besan mixture into the pan, stirring continuously to avoid curdling.
Cook on low heat, stirring often, for about 5–7 minutes.
The besan will start to cook and thicken the gravy.
Keep stirring so it doesn’t stick to the pan.

instruction.title: Step 5: Add Water & Simmer
steps: Add water little by little to reach your desired gravy consistency.
Mix well and let it simmer for 5–10 minutes on low heat.
The gravy should be smooth, thick but pourable.

instruction.title: Step 6: Adjust Seasoning
steps: Add salt to taste and adjust spices if necessary.

instruction.title: Step 7: Garnish & Serve
steps: Garnish with chopped fresh coriander leaves.
Serve hot with steamed rice, roti, or naan.

Serving Suggestions: Serve hot with steamed rice, roti, or naan.
Can be used as a base for vegetable curries, paneer, or even meat preparations.

chefTips: Always add the yogurt-besan mix slowly and stir continuously to prevent curdling.
Use fresh, thick yogurt for the best texture.
Besan needs to be cooked well to avoid the raw taste; keep the heat low to medium to prevent burning.
You can add vegetables like potatoes, peas, or cauliflower to make a vegetable curry.
For a richer flavor, substitute some oil with ghee.
